title: Boat Rental in Belgrade Danube: A 2026 Guide to Sava and Danube Waters
html:
Exploring Belgrade from the water is a revelation, with the confluence of the Sava and Danube rivers offering a unique urban boating experience. Depart from the bustling Marina Dorcol, where you can rent a motorboat for around €250 per half-day, and discover why this stretch of the Danube is a must-visit for travellers. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners to make it simple to book a boat online.

Where to set off in Belgrade Danube

Belgrade’s riverfront is dotted with marinas and landing stages that make launching a boat straightforward. Marina Dorcol, the city’s premier boating hub, sits at the confluence of the Sava and Danube. The Ada Huja marina is popular for swimming and water sports. For a quieter option, try the marina at Zemun, a historic town just upstream. Boaters heading to the Great War Island often anchor off the sandy banks near the village of Krnjača. You can book a boat online in a few minutes.

What are the best things to do by boat in Belgrade Danube?

A boat trip on the Danube in Belgrade combines city sightseeing with serene nature exploration, offering everything from historical landmarks to beach bars. Cruise past the Kalemegdan Fortress, then head upstream to the wooded shores of the Great War Island. Stop at a floating restaurant on the Sava for local fish stew. You can also moor at Lido Beach on the Danube for a swim in summer. Many boaters take a sunset run past the Branko’s Bridge to photograph the illuminated fortress. One option worth a look is the Azimut 58 for hire.

Which boat to rent

  1. Azimut 58, a luxury motor yacht ideal for corporate events or special occasions, sleeps 10, from €1,200 per day. Check the Azimut 58 for hire on BoatHire24.
  2. Axopar 28, a versatile day cruiser perfect for exploring the Sava’s narrow channels, takes 8, from €400 per half-day.
  3. Lagoon 42 catamaran, stable and spacious for family sailing on the river, accommodates 12, from €800 per day.
  4. Fairline Squadron 50, a flybridge motorboat for extended weekends up to the Danube bends, sleeps 6, from €950 per day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Belgrade Danube?

Boat rental prices in Belgrade Danube range from €80 per person for a shared tour to over €1,500 per day for a crewed luxury yacht. The following price bands give a realistic overview: You can also see more at check availability.

  • Shared sightseeing tour: €80 per person, 1.5 hours
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): €250, €350, without skipper
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): €700, €1,000, plus fuel
  • Crewed luxury yacht (Azimut 58) full-day: €1,200, €1,600, including skipper and crew

Always check the advertised price includes VAT and mooring fees. You can book a boat online to compare exact prices for your dates. To see how booking works, visit how boat rental works.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Belgrade Danube?

For most powerboats up to 15m and under 30 knots, an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or a national licence recognised by Serbian authorities is required. Serbian law mandates a licence for any motor vessel over 5hp; the operator will confirm exact requirements at booking. A blockquote best practice: For wind conditions, the high season runs from June to September, when the local 'košava' wind can pick up abruptly from the southeast, so check forecasts before venturing onto the main river.

If you plan to explore the lower Danube gorges in early summer, the košava wind can be strong; always moor stern-to at floating docks to avoid damage.

At a glance

  • Price band: €80, €1,600 per person/trip
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: 1.5 hours to 8 hours
  • Guest capacity: 1, 12 passengers
  • Currency: EUR (cash widely accepted; cards at larger marinas)

Questions about boat rental in Belgrade Danube

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Belgrade?

Yes, you can hire a skippered boat for which no licence is required, or book a small guided tour on the Sava river.

What is the best time of year for a boat trip on the Danube in Belgrade?

The best time is from June to September, when the weather is warm and river cafés and floating clubs are open.

Is it possible to anchor overnight on the Danube in Belgrade?

Overnight anchoring is permitted in designated areas such as the Great War Island lagoon, but you should check with the harbour master.

How far is it from Belgrade to Novi Sad by boat?

The distance from Belgrade to Novi Sad on the Danube is approximately 80 nautical miles, a full day cruise one-way.
